description_long: This object is a tall, industrial-style emergency safety shower and eyewash station, commonly found in laboratories, factories, or urban public spaces. It features a vertical black metal pipe structure anchored to a circular base, with a large green metal shower head at the top and a round eyewash basin with yellow nozzles mounted at mid-height. The shower head is operated by a pull rod hanging down, while the eyewash station is activated by a push plate or lever. There are additional small fixtures, such as a sign plate and a control box, attached to the main pipe. The overall construction is robust, with visible pipe joints and fittings, designed for durability and high visibility in emergency situations. The green and yellow color scheme enhances its recognizability as a safety fixture.
description: A tall, industrial emergency safety shower and eyewash station with a green shower head, yellow eyewash nozzles, and a sturdy black metal pipe frame.
description_view_0: Front view showing the green shower head, eyewash basin, and attached sign plate clearly aligned along the vertical pipe.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the protruding green shower head, the pull rod, and the round eyewash basin with visible plumbing connections.
description_view_2: Rear view with the green shower head and eyewash basin visible, as well as the control box and sign plate on the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the shower head, eyewash basin, and the pull rod, with pipe joints and fittings more prominent.
